```json
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The section interleaves method description, loss formulation, meta-gradient training, and related-work claims; logical consistency between these sub-parts (e.g., whether the stated training alternation is coherent with the meta-gradient derivation and the residual-adaptive sampling) is centrally at risk and relevant to any substantive help.",
    "R1.3": "Multiple specific empirical claims are attributed to named citations (Krishnapriyan 2021, Wang et al. 2022, Rahaman 2019, Tancik 2020, Wang et al. 2021, Wu 2023); verifying that each citation actually supports its paired claim is a core responsibility when helping with a section dense with attributed claims.",
    "R1.6": "'Help me with this section' most commonly signals writing-quality improvement; the prose mixes terse math with narrative and has stylistic variability (e.g., abrupt tense shifts, paragraph-level cohesion gaps) that warrant sentence/paragraph-level attention.",
    "R1.7": "Six distinct citations are embedded; checking date accuracy, author lists, and whether the referenced contributions match how they are characterized (e.g., Wang et al. 2022 for gradient balancing vs. Wang et al. 2021 for NTK/Fourier analysis) is load-bearing given how easily these blur in PINN literature."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I focus primarily on improving prose quality and writing clarity (R1.6), or on reviewing technical and logical correctness of the method description and citations (R1.2, R1.3, R1.7)?"
}
```

rationale.R1.2: The section interleaves method description, loss formulation, meta-gradient training, and related-work claims; logical consistency between these sub-parts (e.g., whether the stated training alternation is coherent with the meta-gradient derivation and the residual-adaptive sampling) is centrally at risk and relevant to any substantive help.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple specific empirical claims are attributed to named citations (Krishnapriyan 2021, Wang et al. 2022, Rahaman 2019, Tancik 2020, Wang et al. 2021, Wu 2023); verifying that each citation actually supports its paired claim is a core responsibility when helping with a section dense with attributed claims.
rationale.R1.6: 'Help me with this section' most commonly signals writing-quality improvement; the prose mixes terse math with narrative and has stylistic variability (e.g., abrupt tense shifts, paragraph-level cohesion gaps) that warrant sentence/paragraph-level attention.
rationale.R1.7: Six distinct citations are embedded; checking date accuracy, author lists, and whether the referenced contributions match how they are characterized (e.g., Wang et al. 2022 for gradient balancing vs. Wang et al. 2021 for NTK/Fourier analysis) is load-bearing given how easily these blur in PINN literature.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on improving prose quality and writing clarity (R1.6), or on reviewing technical and logical correctness of the method description and citations (R1.2, R1.3, R1.7)?
